prompt: Wednesday night the reindex job hung on a wedged NFS mount and the ENTIRE nightly batch sat behind it until morning — nothing after it ran, no alert, nothing. jobs.py needs a grown-up entry point. Keep run() exactly as it is (the cron wrapper's error handling depends on JobFailed), and add execute(timeout=None, continue_on_error=False) that never raises for job outcomes and instead returns a summary I can log and page on. Per record I want name, status ('ok' / 'error' / 'timeout'), the return value for ok (None otherwise), and for 'error' the actual exception object that was raised (None for ok/timeout). The summary also needs .records in registration order, .ok / .errors / .timeouts as name lists, and .all_ok which is only True when every registered job finished ok. Semantics: jobs still run one at a time in order; timeout is per job in seconds (None = no limit, zero or negative = ValueError) and a job that exceeds it is marked 'timeout' and execute MOVES ON immediately — don't wait for the stuck thing, and with continue_on_error=False any non-ok result stops the batch right there (jobs after it must not start, so their records simply don't exist). Threads are fine for the timeout mechanics, just keep results deterministic. Acceptance tests: python3 test_jobs.py — hands off the test file, and don't break the existing run() tests in there.
